description: Pre-selected quality compounders for bear market deployment
deployment_philosophy: Select during bull markets, buy during bear markets when 8+ indicators trigger. Heavy deployment, not DCA.

categories.name: Vertical Market Software
categories.rationale: High switching costs, recurring revenue, capital-light compounding. Fisher quality + Lindy durability.

positions.name: Constellation Software
positions.thesis: VMS roll-up pioneer. 30-year track record of disciplined M&A at 25%+ ROIC. Burgiss, Lerro leadership. Decentralized operating model. Quality compounds forever.
positions.entry_trigger: 8+ indicators or CSU down >25% from peak

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects CSU to grow FCF at 12-14% annually for 10 years, then 3% perpetually.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If multiple compresses from 35x to 25x over 10 years, need 16-18% FCF growth just to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. VMS TAM expanding (cloud migration, vertical deepening). $50B+ addressable.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Historical 20%+ FCF growth (2010-2020). M&A pipeline remains robust. Pricing power intact.
three_ps.probable: 15-18% FCF CAGR. Base case: 15%. Bull case: 18%. Track record supports this.
three_ps.assessment: Probable (15-18%) > Implied (12-14%). Stock is reasonably priced, not expensive.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: Price < CAD 2,200 (Probable growth 20%+ above Implied) OR 8+ bear signals
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Price > CAD 3,500 (Implied > 20%, multiple >40x) AND Probable cannot support it
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: HOLD (slightly undervalued, wait for 8+ bear signals or deeper dip)

positions.name: Topicus.com
positions.thesis: CSU spinoff focused on European VMS. Robin van Poelje leadership. Same playbook, earlier stage, smaller scale. Geographic diversification from CSU.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 14-16% FCF growth. Higher than CSU due to earlier stage, smaller base.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If multiple compresses to 22x (discount to CSU as smaller spinoff), need 18-20% growth to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. European VMS TAM $20B+. Less saturated than North America. Consolidation opportunity.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Following CSU playbook. M&A deals closing. Revenue growth 15%+ since spinoff.
three_ps.probable: 16-20% FCF CAGR. Base case: 16% (slower than early CSU). Bull case: 20% (execution matches CSU).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (16-20%) > Implied (14-16%). Slightly undervalued for quality. Smaller than CSU = higher risk.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: Price < CAD 80 (Probable significantly above Implied) OR 8+ bear signals + price < CAD 90
entry_exit_trigger.hold: CAD 80 - 110 (fair value range given growth + risk)
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Price > CAD 130 (P/FCF >35x, too expensive for spinoff with 5-year track record)
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: HOLD (slightly cheap but wait for 8+ bear signals to add)

positions.name: Lumine Group
positions.thesis: CSU spinoff focused on communication/media VMS. Smaller than TOI, higher risk. Portfolio diversification play.
positions.notes: Already at target weight. Monitor only.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 13-15% FCF growth. Lower multiple than CSU/TOI reflects higher risk, smaller scale.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If multiple compresses to 18x (smallest spinoff, highest risk), need 17-19% to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. Communication/media VMS niche. TAM ~$10B. Narrower than CSU/TOI but defensible.
three_ps.plausible: Maybe. Track record only 3 years. M&A execution unproven. Revenue volatile. Smaller deal flow.
three_ps.probable: 12-16% FCF CAGR. Base case: 12% (execution challenges). Bull case: 16% (if M&A accelerates).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (12-16%) ≈ Implied (13-15%). Fairly valued given elevated risk. Not adding more.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: Not buying. Already at target 5%. Would only add if price < CAD 16 AND 10+ bear signals (extreme dislocation).
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Price > CAD 32 (P/FCF >35x, unjustified for smallest spinoff) OR thesis breaks (M&A stalls)
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: HOLD (at target weight, monitor execution, no plans to add)

categories.name: Enduring Luxury
categories.rationale: Lindy effect: survived centuries. Pricing power, brand moats, generational wealth preservation. Anti-fragile to inflation.

positions.name: Hermès International
positions.thesis: 300+ year family business. Birkin bags, silk scarves. Supply-constrained luxury (artisan bottleneck = pricing power). 30%+ ROIC, no debt. Lindy king.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 8-10% EPS growth for 10 years at premium multiple (luxury scarcity premium).

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If multiple compresses from 55x to 40x, need 12-14% EPS growth to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. Ultra-high-net-worth (UHNW) wealth growing 6-8% annually. China/Asia demand underpenetrated.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Historical 10-12% revenue growth, margin expansion from pricing power. Artisan constraint limits volume.
three_ps.probable: 10-12% EPS CAGR. Pricing power + slow volume expansion. Track record: 11% revenue CAGR (2010-2020).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (10-12%) ≈ Implied (8-10%). Fairly valued at current multiple.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: Price < EUR 2,000 (Probable > Implied by 20%+) OR 8+ bear signals + price < EUR 2,200
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Price > EUR 3,000 (P/E >65x, Implied >12%, unsustainable for slow-growth luxury)
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: HOLD (fairly valued, wait for 8+ bear signals for entry)

positions.name: Ferrari
positions.thesis: Luxury automaker (not car company). Artificial scarcity, 2-year waitlists. 35%+ EBIT margins. Pricing power > volume growth. Survived 75+ years.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 10-12% EPS growth. Premium multiple reflects luxury brand moat + scarcity model.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If multiple compresses from 50x to 35x (still premium but lower), need 15-17% EPS to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. UHNW demand robust. Ferrari can grow units 3-5%/year without diluting brand. Hybrid/EV transition pricing power.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Volume growth 5-7% (waitlist backlog). Price increases 5-8% (inflation + demand). Margins stable/expanding.
three_ps.probable: 12-15% EPS CAGR. Base case: 12% (volume + pricing). Bull case: 15% (personalization upsells + margin expansion).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (12-15%) > Implied (10-12%). Stock fairly valued to slightly cheap. Luxury scarcity moat strong.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: Price < USD 400 (Probable 20%+ above Implied) OR 8+ bear signals + price < USD 450
entry_exit_trigger.hold: USD 400 - 550 (fair value for luxury brand with pricing power)
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Price > USD 650 (P/E >65x, Implied >18%, difficult to sustain for auto-adjacent business)
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: HOLD (fairly valued, add on dislocation when 8+ bear signals)

positions.name: Exor N.V.
positions.thesis: Holding company: Ferrari, Stellantis, Iveco, CNH, Juventus, The Economist, PartnerRe. Discount to NAV (~20-30%). Agnelli family skin in game.
positions.entry_trigger: 9+ indicators (holding company discount needs stronger signal)
positions.notes: Already above target. Monitor discount to NAV.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 6-8% NAV growth (blend of Ferrari growth + Stellantis/CNH mature businesses). Discount persists.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If holding discount widens from 25% to 35% (illiquidity/governance concerns), need 10-12% NAV growth to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. Ferrari (30% of NAV) can compound at 12-15%. Stellantis/CNH/Iveco mature but generate cash. PartnerRe insurance float.
three_ps.plausible: Maybe. Ferrari thesis strong. Stellantis challenged (EV transition, competition). CNH/Iveco cyclical. Holding discount unlikely to close.
three_ps.probable: 6-9% NAV CAGR. Base case: 6% (Ferrari + mature businesses offset). Bull case: 9% (if Stellantis stabilizes).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (6-9%) ≈ Implied (6-8%). Fairly valued given persistent discount. Holding companies trade at discount for a reason.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: Not buying more. Already 6.8% (above 5% target). Would only add if discount widens to 35%+ AND 10+ bear signals.
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Sell partial if price > EUR 140 (discount < 15%, unlikely but would trim) OR Stellantis thesis breaks (EV losses spiral).
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: HOLD / TRIM (above target weight, monitor NAV composition, consider trimming on strength)

categories.name: Technology Compounders
categories.rationale: Wide moats, network effects, high ROIC. Dominant platforms with pricing power.

positions.name: Alphabet Inc.
positions.thesis: Search monopoly (90% market share). YouTube moat. Cloud #3. AI positioning. 30%+ ROIC, fortress balance sheet. Survived 25+ years.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 10-12% EPS growth. Lower multiple than MSFT reflects antitrust risk, AI disruption fears.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If multiple compresses to 22x (mature big tech), need 14-16% EPS to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. Search TAM still growing (mobile, voice). YouTube shorts = new ad inventory. Cloud growing 20-30%. AI monetization early.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Search ad revenue stable. YouTube revenue growth 10-15%. Cloud margin expansion. AI integration across products (Search, Workspace, Cloud).
three_ps.probable: 12-15% EPS CAGR. Base case: 12% (Search matures, Cloud compensates). Bull case: 15% (AI accelerates Search + Cloud).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (12-15%) > Implied (10-12%). Stock undervalued due to antitrust fears. High-quality moat trading cheap.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: Price < USD 160 (Probable 20%+ above Implied, deep value) OR 8+ bear signals + price < USD 175
entry_exit_trigger.hold: USD 160 - 210 (fair value range for quality + moat)
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Price > USD 250 (P/E >35x, Implied >15%, hard to sustain for $2T company)
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: BUY / ADD (undervalued at 28x, target 8% allocation, deploy when 8+ bear signals OR price drops further)

positions.name: Adobe Inc.
positions.thesis: Creative software monopoly. Subscription model lock-in. 35%+ ROIC. Photoshop/Illustrator = industry standard for 30+ years.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 11-13% EPS growth. Creative Cloud subscription base maturing, AI tools (Firefly) monetization early.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If multiple compresses to 25x (mature SaaS), need 15-17% EPS to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. Creative market TAM $30B+. AI content creation tools expanding TAM. Enterprise + SMB penetration room to grow.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Subscription model sticky (95%+ retention). Price increases 5-10% every 2-3 years. Firefly AI upsells beginning. Figma acquisition blocked but organic growth continues.
three_ps.probable: 10-13% EPS CAGR. Base case: 10% (subscriptions mature, AI offsets). Bull case: 13% (Firefly accelerates, enterprise adoption).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (10-13%) ≈ Implied (11-13%). Fairly valued at current multiple. Not expensive but not cheap.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: Price < USD 380 (Probable 15%+ above Implied) OR 8+ bear signals + price < USD 410
entry_exit_trigger.hold: USD 380 - 480 (fair value for subscription moat + pricing power)
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Price > USD 550 (P/E >40x, Implied >16%, difficult for maturing SaaS)
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: HOLD (near fair value, will add when 8+ bear signals to reach 4% target)

positions.name: Microsoft
positions.thesis: Enterprise moat (Office, Azure, Windows). 40%+ ROIC. OpenAI partnership. Survived 50+ years, still compounding.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 12-14% EPS growth for 10 years, driven by Azure + AI monetization.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If multiple compresses from 38x to 28x (mature tech), need 16-18% EPS growth to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. Cloud TAM $1T+. Enterprise software moat defensible. AI tailwinds real.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Azure growing 25-30%. Office/Windows stable cash cows. AI integration across product suite.
three_ps.probable: 14-16% EPS CAGR. Base case: 14% (Azure moderates, margin expansion). Bull case: 16% (AI accelerates).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (14-16%) ≈ Implied (12-14%). Fairly to slightly overvalued at current multiple.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: Price < USD 380 (Probable >20% above Implied) OR 8+ bear signals + price < USD 420
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Price > USD 550 (P/E >45x, Implied >18%, difficult to sustain for mega-cap)
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: WAIT (fairly valued, not a buy unless 8+ bear signals trigger)

categories.name: Broad Market Exposure
categories.rationale: Core recovery plays. Get the beta, reduce single-stock risk. Safe deployment when signals fire.

positions.name: Vanguard FTSE All-World ETF
positions.thesis: Global market cap weighted. Lowest cost, broadest diversification. US + ex-US exposure. Mean reversion to long-term growth.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 7-9% global earnings growth (GDP growth + margin expansion). Blended US + ex-US.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If global P/E compresses from 20x to 16x (historical average), need 11-13% earnings growth to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. Global GDP growing 2.5-3.5%. Corporate earnings historically grow 1-2% above GDP. Market cap weighted = winners compound.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Long-term historical EPS growth 6-8% for global equities. US tech dominance drives higher weighted average.
three_ps.probable: 7-10% EPS CAGR. Base case: 7% (GDP + modest margin expansion). Bull case: 10% (tech/AI drives above-trend growth).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (7-10%) ≈ Implied (7-9%). Fairly valued. Buy on any dislocation (8+ bear signals).

entry_exit_trigger.buy: 7+ bear signals (market oversold, safe deployment). Price drops are buying opportunities (mean reversion).
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Never sell VWRA (hold forever, core position). Only trim if allocation exceeds 30% due to outperformance.
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: HOLD (at target weight, deploy more when 7+ bear signals trigger)

positions.name: SPDR S&P 500 ETF
positions.thesis: US large-cap exposure. Complement to VWRA. Mean reversion play when market oversold.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 9-11% S&P 500 earnings growth. Higher than global average due to US tech weight.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If S&P P/E compresses from 23x to 18x (historical average), need 13-15% earnings growth to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. US corporate earnings historically grow 6-8%. Tech dominance (30% of S&P) drives higher growth.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Mega-cap tech (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA) growing 10-15%. Rest of S&P at 4-6%. Blended = 8-10%.
three_ps.probable: 8-11% EPS CAGR. Base case: 8% (tech moderates). Bull case: 11% (AI drives mega-cap earnings surprise).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (8-11%) ≈ Implied (9-11%). Fairly valued at 23x. Not cheap but not expensive. Buy on dislocation.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: 8+ bear signals + S&P down >20% from peak. Mean reversion trade. Target entry: SPY < USD 500.
entry_exit_trigger.hold: USD 500 - 650 (fair value range for US large-cap)
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Never sell SPY (buy-and-hold). Only trim if allocation exceeds 10% due to outperformance.
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: WAIT (not buying at all-time highs, deploy when 8+ bear signals trigger)

positions.name: Invesco QQQ Trust
positions.thesis: Tech-heavy Nasdaq 100. Higher beta, bigger recoveries. Buy during panic for outsized gains.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 12-14% Nasdaq 100 earnings growth. Premium valuation reflects tech dominance + growth.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If Nasdaq P/E compresses from 32x to 24x (closer to S&P), need 16-18% earnings growth to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. Mega-cap tech (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, AMZN, META, TSLA) = 50% of QQQ. These can grow 10-15% for years.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Cloud, AI, digital ads, SaaS all have multi-year tailwinds. Regulatory risk manageable.
three_ps.probable: 12-16% EPS CAGR. Base case: 12% (tech growth moderates). Bull case: 16% (AI drives mega-cap earnings acceleration).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (12-16%) ≈ Implied (12-14%). Fairly valued at 32x. Buy on panic, not at highs.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: 9+ bear signals + Nasdaq down >30% from peak. High-beta recovery play. Target entry: QQQ < USD 420.
entry_exit_trigger.hold: USD 420 - 580 (fair value for tech concentration + growth)
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Never sell QQQ (buy-and-hold tech exposure). Only trim if allocation exceeds 8% due to outperformance.
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: WAIT (not buying at highs, need 9+ bear signals + significant drawdown)

categories.name: Other Compounders
categories.rationale: Opportunistic high-quality names. Add when thesis + signals align.

positions.name: Berkshire Hathaway
positions.thesis: Buffett fortress. Insurance float, diversified holdings. Survives everything. Buy when trading at discount to intrinsic value.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 9-11% book value growth. Blend of operating earnings (insurance, utilities, BNSF) + equity portfolio appreciation.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If P/B compresses to 1.2x (closer to long-term average), need 12-14% book growth to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. Berkshire has $150B+ cash earning 5%+ (float + treasuries). Operating businesses generate $30B+ annual cash flow. Equity portfolio (AAPL, BAC, KO) compounds.
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Historical book value CAGR 10-12% (1965-2020). Buybacks when P/B < 1.2. Operating businesses stable.
three_ps.probable: 8-11% Book CAGR. Base case: 8% (post-Buffett uncertainty). Bull case: 11% (if capital allocation remains disciplined).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (8-11%) ≈ Implied (9-11%). Fairly valued at 1.6x book. Buffett succession risk priced in.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: P/B < 1.3 (Buffett's buyback threshold) AND 8+ bear signals. Target entry: BRK.B < USD 420.
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Price > USD 600 (P/B >1.8, expensive for Berkshire) OR Buffett/Munger succession botched (capital allocation deteriorates).
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: WAIT (at 1.6x book, not cheap enough yet, wait for 8+ bear signals + P/B < 1.3)

positions.name: Costco Wholesale
positions.thesis: Membership moat. 90%+ renewal rates. Low-margin, high-volume model = pricing power during inflation. 40-year track record.

reverse_dcf.interpretation: Market expects 12-14% EPS growth. Premium multiple reflects membership moat + defensive business model + inflation beneficiary.

multiple_compression_scenario.interpretation: If P/E compresses from 55x to 40x (still premium but lower), need 16-18% EPS to break even.

three_ps.possible: Yes. US/Canada penetration high but international (China, Europe) room to grow. Membership fees provide 70%+ of net income (high-margin recurring revenue).
three_ps.plausible: Yes. Same-store sales growing 5-8%. New warehouse openings 20-30/year. Membership fee increases every 5-7 years (last: 2024). E-commerce growing.
three_ps.probable: 10-13% EPS CAGR. Base case: 10% (mature US, slower international). Bull case: 13% (if international accelerates + fee increases).
three_ps.assessment: Probable (10-13%) < Implied (12-14%). Stock expensive at 55x P/E. Only buy on significant dislocation.

entry_exit_trigger.buy: 9+ bear signals + price < USD 700 (P/E <40x, Probable ≈ Implied). Need extreme dislocation to justify entry.
entry_exit_trigger.hold: USD 700 - 950 (fair value for quality + moat, but still pricey)
entry_exit_trigger.sell: Price > USD 1,100 (P/E >65x, Implied >16%, unsustainable for retail)
entry_exit_trigger.current_signal: WAIT (too expensive at 55x, need major correction + 9+ bear signals to consider)

deployment_notes: Total target allocation: 100% when fully deployed
Dry powder target: Deploy when 8+ bear market indicators trigger
Deployment style: Heavy, concentrated buys. Not DCA.
Rebalancing: No. This is additive deployment of new cash only.
Exit strategy: Hold forever (or until thesis breaks). Not selling during bear markets.
